name: Marketing Strategy
summary: Comprehensive go-to-market strategy arc structured around the 4Ps. Use for category launches, annual marketing plans, and any deck where the audience needs to see customer, market, competition, and tactics in one coherent picture.

beats.name: Introduction
beats.description: Open by establishing the product or service this strategy is for, and what window of time it covers. One slide that tells the audience exactly which world they're being asked to weigh in on.

beats.name: Objective
beats.description: State the marketing goals in measurable terms: revenue, pipeline, awareness, share. Two or three goals, ranked. Goals without ranking get equal energy and equal mediocrity.

beats.name: Customer
beats.description: Detail the target audience. Segments, jobs-to-be-done, decision criteria, where they live in the buying cycle. Specificity here is the foundation everything else stands on.

beats.name: Market
beats.description: Describe the market landscape: size, growth, segments, behavioral trends. Use external data for credibility and your own research for nuance. Show how the market is changing, not just how big it is.

beats.name: Competition
beats.description: Map the competitive set honestly. Direct, indirect, and substitutes. A 2x2 or feature-comparison framework helps; what matters is that the audience trusts your read of the field.

beats.name: Strategy
beats.description: Articulate the marketing approach in one sentence and a single supporting frame. Positioning, primary motion, where you choose to play and not play. Clarity here is what makes the tactics that follow coherent.

beats.name: Product
beats.description: Highlight the unique value the product brings to the strategy. Lead with the customer outcome; tie it directly back to the customer beat. Avoid feature soup.

beats.name: Price
beats.description: Justify pricing and packaging. Show the logic — cost-plus, value-based, competitor-anchored — and the levers you'll use over the period. Price decisions are strategy decisions.

beats.name: Place
beats.description: Describe distribution channels and the role each plays. Direct, partner, marketplace, retail. Channel mix is often where strategies quietly succeed or fail.

beats.name: Promotion
beats.description: Reveal the campaign and content tactics that bring the strategy to market. Pair each tactic with the audience and metric it serves. Avoid listing channels for their own sake.

beats.name: Summary
beats.description: Recap the strategy in a single page: customer, position, motion, expected outcome. End with the decision or commitment you need from the audience.
